fingerprint: CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic dependency: the dome's confirmation data is computed from spherical harmonic coefficients (eigenfunctions of the Laplacian on a sphere), and the 50.57° angular separation is measured in spherical coordinates that distort by 2.2× under the dome's own AE projection
globe_value: CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model: Bᵣ(r,θ,φ) = -∇Φ where Φ = R_E Σ(R_E/r)^(n+1) Σ [gₙᵐcos(mφ) + hₙᵐsin(mφ)] Pₙᵐ(cosθ). Spherical harmonics Yₙᵐ are eigenfunctions of the angular Laplacian on a sphere — they satisfy ∇²Yₙᵐ = -n(n+1)Yₙᵐ/R². No flat-disc equivalent exists: on a disc, the eigenfunctions are Bessel functions Jₘ(kρ), which produce qualitatively different spatial patterns.
dome_value: SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg — hardcoded constant in model page (inject_ai_layer.py). No formula derives this from dome parameters. Data source is CHAOS-7 (spherical harmonic expansion through degree ~20), which the dome treats as raw observation while discarding the spherical geometry that produced it.
match_precision: N/A — structural dependency, not a single-number coincidence
why_it_matters: Three interlocking globe dependencies: (1) DATA SOURCE: CHAOS-7 decomposes the geomagnetic field into spherical harmonic coefficients up to degree ~20. These coefficients encode the 3D structure of a spherical Earth — they are solutions to the Laplace equation in spherical coordinates. The reversed-flux patches that cause SAA bifurcation are identified as specific (n,m) mode contributions. A flat disc would have Bessel-function eigenmodes that produce different spatial patterns (concentric rings vs. continent-shaped anomalies). The dome imports CHAOS-7 outputs while rejecting the spherical geometry that generated them. (2) COORDINATE DISTORTION: The '50.57 degrees' is measured in globe longitude. At the SAA's latitude (~25°S), 50.57° of longitude spans ~5,100 km on the globe surface. On the dome's AE projection (r = 20,015 × colatitude/180°), the same angular separation at the same latitude maps to ~11,290 km — a 2.2× inflation. The dome quotes the globe's angular measurement without correcting for its own geometry, producing internally inconsistent distance claims. (3) VORTEX DECAY PHYSICS: If SAA separation truly follows exponential decay, the time constant should depend on the geometry. On a sphere, diffusive decay of mode n goes as exp(-n(n+1)ν t/R²). On a flat disc, it goes as exp(-αₙₘ² ν t/a²) where αₙₘ are Bessel zeros. These give different ratios between modes. The dome claims 'exponential' without specifying any time constant derivable from dome parameters — because the only physically motivated time constant requires spherical geometry.
suggested_text: The dome's claimed confirmation relies on CHAOS-7 data, which decomposes Earth's magnetic field using spherical harmonic coefficients — eigenfunctions of the Laplacian on a sphere. These mathematical objects encode the three-dimensional structure of a spherical planet; on a flat disc, the equivalent eigenfunctions would be Bessel functions with qualitatively different spatial patterns. The dome imports CHAOS-7's globe-computed outputs as raw observation while rejecting the spherical geometry that produced them. Moreover, the quoted '50.57° angular separation' is a spherical longitude measurement: at the SAA's latitude, this spans ~5,100 km on the globe but ~11,290 km under the dome's own AE projection — a 2.2× distortion the dome does not acknowledge.
